company_profile.company_profile_text:
Who We Are
Meridian Intelligence GmbH is a Berlin-based research and data analytics consultancy specialising in market intelligence for EU institutional clients and large-scale private sector organisations. We help clients understand complex, fragmented markets — identifying who the relevant actors are, how they are distributed, how markets are evolving, and what the evidence base is for regulatory or strategic decisions.
We combine proprietary web-based data infrastructure with rigorous research methodology to produce market intelligence that goes beyond what commercial databases can provide. Our work is particularly suited to niche and fragmented markets where standard classification systems fail to capture the relevant population — sectors like cybersecurity, data services, AI, digital media, fintech, and advanced manufacturing.
Since 2016, we have delivered more than 40 completed projects for EU institutional clients, with a cumulative dataset volume exceeding 80 million organisational web records processed.

Our Services
Market and Ecosystem Mapping
We identify and profile all relevant organisations in a defined market — including actors that are invisible to standard databases because they are small, niche, or not self-labelled. Our mapping methodology combines large-scale web data collection, automated classification, and manual verification to produce structured, machine-readable entity datasets with full indicator profiles.
Continuous Market Monitoring
We maintain live monitoring capabilities for client-defined markets, delivering structured updates on new entrants, exits, portfolio changes, and ecosystem shifts. Update cadences range from weekly (for fast-moving markets) to quarterly (for stable sectors). All updates include structured change logs.
Regulatory and Policy Research
We support EU institutions and member state bodies in translating regulatory frameworks into empirical analytical scopes. This includes defining which entities fall within a regulation's coverage, operationalising legal definitions as classification criteria, and producing the evidence base for impact assessments and policy evaluations.
Supply Chain and Network Analysis
We map relationships between organisations in a defined ecosystem — supplier-customer relationships, subcontracting chains, ownership networks, and collaboration links. Our supply chain analysis is derived from web evidence and procurement data rather than self-reported survey responses.

Our Approach
The core of Meridian's methodology is what we call evidence-based organisation profiling: the systematic identification and classification of organisations using observable, verifiable signals rather than self-reported classifications or generic keyword matching.

We start with the broadest possible universe of candidates — typically all organisations with a web presence in the relevant geography.
We then apply a cascade of classification filters, each requiring a specific type of hard evidence to retain a candidate in scope.
We run automated quality checks and a manual verification pass on a statistical sample of each delivered dataset.
We track and publish our false-positive rate, coverage rate, and validation statistics with every major deliverable.

This approach consistently outperforms database-lookup methods in markets where the relevant actors are not centrally registered or do not self-identify with standard industry labels — which is the case in the majority of our clients' markets of interest.

Available on request for ongoing contracts
Our Methodology: WebMap
WebMap is Meridian's proprietary five-stage pipeline for large-scale organisational profiling. It differs from
database-lookup approaches in one fundamental respect: entities are included only if verifiable evidence
supports their relevance. We do not infer relevance from NACE codes or generic keywords.
Stage
What happens

1 — Seed universe
Ingest from company registries (Tier 1) + proprietary web index (Tier 2) + sector registries (Tier 3). EU27
coverage starting from 2–12M candidates.
2 — Deduplication
VAT-number matching → fuzzy name matching (Jaro-Winkler ≥ 0.92) → domain-level consolidation.
Duplicate rate post-dedup: <1.5%.
3 — Web data
collection
Crawl primary web domain. Extract text from About

Team pages. Archive with
timestamp.
4 — Evidence
classification
NLP-based classifier scores each candidate against scope criteria. Hard evidence required for inclusion.
Reject rate: typically 85–95% of seed universe.
5 — Verification &
profiling
Manual spot-check (5% random sample). Derive structured indicator profiles. False-positive rate in final
delivery: <3%.
Technology
Layer
Stack
Data collection
Scrapy

fine-tuned
BERT classifiers
Entity resolution
dedupe library + custom Jaro-Winkler
Pipeline orchestration
Apache Airflow
Storage & query
PostgreSQL
